topic: Frozen prospective predictions for 2026-08-12 total solar eclipse — pre-OTS-anchor adversarial review
our_argument_summary: EXP-283 is the analyst Mode 5 frozen-prediction artifact for the 2026-08-12 total solar eclipse. The proposed_files[0].contents block is the file that will be OTS-anchored at predictions/eclipse-2026-08-12-predictions.json. It registers three buckets (B1 standard physics, B2 dome-geometry-forced, B3 symmetric falsification including F-OURS) with the review repo and dome repo SHAs pinned in _meta. The frozen file is fundamentally sound — formulas match the dome SHA c3b9f56, the geometric and magnetic calculations reproduce to within rounding, and the symmetric-falsification triad is well-formed. However, six MODERATE findings constitute attack surface for a hostile reader and should be fixed before OTS-anchoring; nine MINOR findings are nice-to-have fixes that mostly become permanent rounding/derivation-prose inconsistencies once the file is anchored.

what_was_reviewed.target_review_was_against: proposed_files[0].contents — the bytes that will be JSON.stringified and committed to predictions/eclipse-2026-08-12-predictions.json
what_was_reviewed.pinned_dome_sha: c3b9f56fe622f6d3162a03671e797fe74fa6859a (verified via GitHub API — date 2026-05-02T00:03:03Z, dome version V51.1)
what_was_reviewed.pinned_dome_formula_runtime_verified: FORM-V51-001 H(r)=8537*exp(-r/8619), FORM-V51-002 n(r)=1+0.20*(8537/H(r)-1), FORM-V51-004 dg=dB/1.672, FORM-V51-005 FSF(lat)=0.19550/sin(lat)^0.1640*32.974^(lat/90) — all match the frozen file verbatim at the pinned SHA

pass_1_absolute.summary: Sources, units, derivability, falsifiability, symmetry. No CRITICAL findings (no typos, no unit errors, no missing-source bare numbers). All numerical claims are sourced. Falsification triad is symmetric (B3-F-DOME, B3-F-STDPHYS, B3-F-OURS all present). Three MODERATE findings about underspecification of falsification thresholds, sign convention, and candidate-station list.

findings.description: OBS-D-02 falsification criterion uses 'observed amplitudes deviate from dome-formula predictions by > 2x the per-station noise floor at a majority of stations' but 'per-station noise floor' is not defined in the frozen file. INTERMAGNET noise floors are station-specific, condition-dependent, and require methodology to compute. A hostile reader can argue we computed the noise floor post-hoc to fit the result.
findings.recommendation: Pre-commit to a specific noise-floor methodology IN THE FROZEN FILE. Two options: (a) numeric — fix the threshold at +/-2 nT for all 9 stations (consistent with INTERMAGNET 1-min H-component RMS noise during quiet conditions); (b) procedural — define noise floor as 2 sigma of H-component variability over the 30 quiet days (Kp <= 2) immediately preceding the eclipse, computed from INTERMAGNET 1-minute data, station-specific. Option (a) is simpler and OTS-cleaner; option (b) more defensible scientifically but requires post-event publication of the 30-day computation.

findings.description: Sign convention inconsistency between B1-M-001 and B2-M-001. B1-M-001 amplitude_range_nt = [5, 25] uses positive numbers ('amplitude of order single-digit-to-low-double-digit nT'). B2-M-001 amplitude_range_nT = [-12.83, -5.04] uses signed negative numbers. The Chapman-Sq physical signature is a NEGATIVE deviation (Sq currents weaken when ionospheric heating drops); the dome's BOU baseline is also negative. Post-event comparison protocol is ambiguous — is observation compared as absolute magnitude against [5, 25] and against [5.04, 12.83], or as signed deviations against [-25, -5] and [-12.83, -5.04]?
findings.recommendation: Pick one convention and apply uniformly. Recommended: state explicitly under both buckets that all amplitudes refer to SIGNED deviations from a quiet-day H-component baseline, with eclipse-suppressed Sq currents producing a NEGATIVE deviation. Restate B1-M-001 as [-25, -5] nT (signed) and harmonize the falsification criteria.

findings.description: OBS-O-03 falsification criterion depends on 'any non-dome-cited high-obscuration INTERMAGNET station' but the candidate set is not pre-specified. After the event, a hostile reader will argue we cherry-picked stations to make the criterion fire (or not fire). The criterion is unfalsifiable in practice without a pre-committed station list.
findings.recommendation: Pre-list specific candidate non-dome-cited high-obscuration INTERMAGNET stations IN THE FROZEN FILE. Suggested candidates within the August 2026 partial-coverage band: Manhay (MAB) Belgium 50.30N 5.68E; Dourbes (DOU) Belgium 50.10N 4.60E; Wingst (WNG) Germany 53.74N 9.07E; Surlari (SUA) Romania 44.68N 26.25E; Belsk (BEL) Poland 51.84N 20.79E. The list locks the comparison set before observation.

findings.description: B1-M-001 prediction text uses 'peak amplitude of order single-digit-to-low-double-digit nT (commonly 5-25 nT)'. The qualifier 'commonly' weakens what should be a hard prediction interval. A hostile reader can claim a 4.8 nT or 27 nT result is in the 'uncommon' range that we silently allowed for.
findings.recommendation: Drop 'commonly'. Either commit to [5, 25] as a hard interval, or widen to literature-supported bounds (Kim and Chang 2018 reports 39-eclipse ensemble with 95 percent within ~3-30 nT) and commit to that wider interval. The interval should be hard, with a separate explicit-range field for falsification.

findings.description: B2-G-001 derivation prose: 'R_u = 12.00 - 14.32 x 2,534 / 3,199 = 0.658 km'. With the displayed inputs, exact arithmetic gives 12.00 - 11.343 = 0.657, not 0.658. The result 0.658 is correct from full-precision inputs (R_Sun = 26.323, R_Moon = 12.001) but the displayed derivation rounds the intermediate to 14.32 (instead of 14.32115). Cosmetic — within +/-0.03 km confidence bound — but in an OTS-anchored file the cosmetic inconsistency lives forever.
findings.recommendation: Either (a) restate the derivation with one more digit on the intermediate ('R_Sun - R_Moon ~= 14.321') or (b) round the result to match the displayed inputs ('~= 0.66 km', giving diameter ~= 1.32 km — which is what 4.2.7 prose actually uses) and use 1.32 km in B2-G-001 instead of 1.317 km. Option (b) aligns the frozen file with the pinned-SHA prose without losing falsification precision.

findings.description: B2-M-001 says 'Per-station central values are exact-arithmetic from dome-published numerical inputs (no scientific uncertainty added)' — but post-event evaluation needs a tolerance to decide match/no-match. The dome's own raw-text 9-station table carries +/-2.1 nT (Ebro), +/-2.6 nT (Hartland), +/-1.0 nT (Canary), etc. error bars that the frozen file does not propagate.
findings.recommendation: Either inherit the dome's stated error bars (+/-1 to +/-2.6 nT per station, station-specific) into B2-M-001 as the falsification tolerance, or commit to a uniform tolerance (+/-2 nT) tied to P1-NF noise-floor pre-commitment.

findings.description: post_event_protocol step 2 says 'Compute per-station peak dB after subtracting a quiet-day baseline (standard preprocessing).' Standard practice but the procedure (which quiet days, which component, regression vs subtraction, time-window for 'peak') is not specified. A hostile reader can argue that whatever protocol we choose post-event was selected to favor our analysis.
findings.recommendation: Pre-register the protocol IN THE FROZEN FILE: e.g., 'H-component, 1-minute INTERMAGNET cadence, baseline = mean of the same UT hour on the 5 quietest days (Kp <= 1) of the 30 days preceding the eclipse, peak = max |dB| in the +/-60 min window centered on local maximum coverage.' Whatever procedure is chosen, freeze it before the data.

pass_2_consistency.summary: Live-repo cross-checks at pinned SHAs. The frozen file is internally consistent with the pinned dome SHA (formulas, version label, baseline) and with the pinned review SHA (geometric values, station table, trilemma framing) — except for one non-trivial penumbra-radius rounding inconsistency between B2-G-004 and B2-M-001 within the frozen file itself. All citation paths resolve. All formulas match. Per-station coverage and FSF values match dome's raw-text exactly.

cross_check_results.review_repo_sha_check: cd8af4b5d9c4e0d182b71ba6734dd0f0310011b5 — verified to exist; commit message 'workspace-sync run report: 2026-05-01T23:05:10Z'. 4.2.7 prose at this SHA matches frozen-file numerics: R_Sun ~ 26.3 km, R_Moon ~ 12.0 km, D = 3,199 km, R_u ~ 0.66 km, R_p ~ 42 km, penumbra band 85 km, transit 87 km/min and 25 km/min, durations 0.9 s lower and ~7 s upper, 138-second observed maximum, 294-km path width — all match.
cross_check_results.dome_repo_sha_check: c3b9f56fe622f6d3162a03671e797fe74fa6859a — verified via GitHub API; date 2026-05-02T00:03:03Z; commit message 'Auto-update ECM status 2026-05-02 00:03:03'. docs/api/current/formula_runtime.json at this SHA shows version V51.1; FORM-V51-001/002/004/005 expressions match the frozen file VERBATIM.
cross_check_results.per_station_table_check: Frozen file's B2-M-001 per_station table reproduces dome raw-text/03-predictions.txt 9-station entries exactly: Ebro 0.95/1.029, San Pablo 0.90/1.001, Eskdalemuir 0.55/1.722, Lerwick 0.42/2.075, Hartland 0.80/1.471, Niemegk 0.40/1.529, Canary 0.72/0.642, Chambon 0.70/1.325, Coimbra 0.92/1.008.
cross_check_results.delta_b_arithmetic_check: Re-computed each station's BOU-baseline dB = -10.9 x coverage x FSF and verified against frozen file's B2-M-001 per_station predictions. All match to 2 decimals: Ebro -10.66, San Pablo -9.82, Eskdalemuir -10.32, Lerwick -9.50, Hartland -12.83, Niemegk -6.67, Canary -5.04, Chambon -10.11, Coimbra -10.11.
cross_check_results.geometric_calc_check: Independent recomputation: R_Sun = 5733 x tan(0.26306 deg) = 26.321 km (file 26.32); R_Moon = 2534 x tan(0.27136 deg) = 12.001 km (file 12.00); D = 3199 km; R_u = 12.00 - (R_Sun - R_Moon) x h_Moon / D = 0.658 km; diameter 1.317 km; R_p = 42.358 km (file 42.4); band width 84.7 km; rim circumference 125,758 km, 87 km/min.
cross_check_results.hartland_88_check: Frozen file B1-G-005 commits Hartland 88 percent obscuration. 4.2.7 prose at pinned SHA states 'at Hartland, partial coverage lasts roughly 2 h 45 min and peaks at ~88 percent obscuration (NASA SE2026Aug12T Besselian elements, Fred Espenak / NASA GSFC, for Hartland 51.0 N, 4.48 W).' Match. The 88 percent value is not independently re-derived in this review — relies on 4.2.7 prose which credits NASA Besselian-elements local-circumstances calculation.

findings.description: Internal numerical inconsistency in penumbra-radius multipliers within the frozen file. B2-G-002 declares R_p = 42.4 km. B2-G-004 closest_station/farthest_station report multiples 10.6x (Ebro 444 km) and 97.2x (Canary 4,084 km), which use R_p = 42 km (matching 4.2.7a prose at pinned SHA: 'ten penumbra-radii away ... ninety-seven penumbra-radii away'). B2-M-001 per_station entries report multiples 10.48x (Ebro), 12.58x (San Pablo), 16.76x (Hartland), ..., 96.41x (Canary), which use R_p = 42.36 km. So the same frozen file uses two different effective R_p values for displaying multipliers — neither of which is the value declared in B2-G-002 (42.4 km). 444/42.4 = 10.47 (not 10.48 or 10.6); 4084/42.4 = 96.32 (not 96.41 or 97.2).
findings.recommendation: Pick one R_p convention and propagate consistently. Suggested: use R_p = 42.4 km (the declared value) and recompute all multipliers (Ebro 10.5x, San Pablo 12.6x, Eskdalemuir 28.7x, Hartland 16.8x, Niemegk 77.0x, Canary 96.3x, Chambon 35.7x, Lerwick 42.6x, Coimbra 23.4x). Alternative: use full-precision R_p = 42.36 km and accept 10.48x / 12.58x / etc. Whichever path is chosen, B2-G-004, B2-M-001, and the section-prose pinned at cd8af4b should agree on a SINGLE convention.

pass_3_trap.summary: Hostile-reader hunt for overcommitment, hidden assumptions, and post-hoc-defensible language. Two MODERATE findings about post-hoc-attackable falsification language; multiple MINOR findings about unstated assumptions.

findings.description: OBS-D-02 says 'if observed amplitudes deviate from dome-formula predictions by > 2x the per-station noise floor at a majority of stations, the dome formula is falsified.' Two attack surfaces: (a) 'per-station noise floor' is undefined (cross-references P1-NF — moderate); (b) 'a majority of stations' — does that mean 5 of 9? 6 of 9? With one or two stations that happen to land at exactly the dome's BOU prediction, the dome author can claim partial corroboration. The criterion mixes a per-station threshold with an aggregate quorum.
findings.recommendation: Replace 'a majority of stations' with a specific count: '>= 5 of the 9 dome-cited stations' (clean majority). And resolve P1-NF noise-floor definition. Better still: state the criterion as a per-station test with no quorum, and add a separate quorum-style claim if needed (e.g., 'if >= 5 of 9 stations show |observed - dome-formula-prediction| > 2 nT under Kp < 2, the dome formula is falsified').

findings.description: OBS-D-03 says 'observed magnetic-signal pattern across the 9 stations tracks NASA per-station obscuration rather than dome's published per-station coverage assignments.' The criterion is qualitative — 'tracks' is not operationalized. A hostile reader will demand: linear regression coefficient threshold? Pearson r? Rank correlation Spearman rho? At what significance level?
findings.recommendation: Operationalize: 'Pearson r between observed peak |dB| (after baseline subtraction per pre-registered protocol) and NASA local-circumstances obscuration percentage exceeds Pearson r between observed peak |dB| and dome-published coverage values, AND the difference is statistically significant at p < 0.05 under a paired two-sample test on the 9 stations.' That gives a yes/no falsification.

findings.description: B3-F-OURS OBS-O-01 says 'BOTH standard-physics predictions AND dome-geometry-forced predictions are corroborated by observation. This is impossible if the 4.2.7 trilemma is correct.' For path-width (B1: 294 km vs B2: 1.317 km) the criterion is geometrically impossible — the world cannot simultaneously corroborate both. So OBS-O-01 is partly vacuous as a retraction trigger on the geometric bucket. For magnetic predictions (B1: |5-25| nT, B2: |5-13| nT) the buckets overlap in the absolute-value range 5-13 nT, so a measurement landing there does not distinguish models — but the criterion as written treats this as 'both corroborated', triggering retraction. That is too aggressive: an observation in the overlap zone simply doesn't discriminate; it doesn't refute our trilemma.
findings.recommendation: Restrict OBS-O-01 to the geometric bucket where mutual corroboration is genuinely impossible (so the criterion is logically empty but symbolically symmetric — fine). Add a separate magnetic-bucket criterion that triggers retraction only if observations are OUTSIDE both predicted ranges in a way that suggests neither model captures the phenomenon. Or accept the current framing but explicitly note in the criterion text that it is mostly a symbolic commitment for path-width and is intentionally non-vacuous for magnetic only when observation lies cleanly outside both ranges.

findings.description: B2-G-004 reports per-station distances using 'AE-from-North azimuthal-equidistant projection with R_disc/90 charity convention' (the 4.2.7a footnote convention). The frozen file mentions 'AE-from-N projection' only in the source_provenance string and in robustness_check_stricter_AE_convention. A hostile reader who hasn't read 4.2.7a may not understand what the convention is or why it's chosen.
findings.recommendation: Add a short 'projection_convention' block under B2-G-004 stating: 'Globe lat/lon mapped to disc radius via azimuthal-equidistant projection from North Pole with R_disc / 90 deg latitude (the charity convention — gives the dome the largest disc radii and thus the shortest distances to the umbra path; under stricter R_disc / 180 deg convention, distances halve). The 0/9 headline survives both conventions.' One paragraph closes the gap.

findings.description: B1-G-005 commits Hartland 88 percent obscuration as the only per-station NASA-anchored value but does not show the derivation (NASA Besselian elements + Hartland coordinates -> 88 percent). The frozen file cites the NASA Besselian-elements page URL and 4.2.7 prose, but a hostile reader must trust 4.2.7's calculation rather than re-derive from primary source.
findings.recommendation: Either (a) add a derivation_notes field with the local-circumstances inputs (Hartland 51.00N, 4.48W; NASA SE2026Aug12T greatest-eclipse moment 17:45:53.8 UT; magnitude 1.0386 at greatest; computed using Espenak's local-circumstances calculator on date YYYY-MM-DD); or (b) link explicitly to a saved screenshot/PDF of the NASA local-circumstances calculator output for Hartland and reference its archived URL. Option (a) is sufficient for re-derivability.

findings.description: Frozen file pins dome SHA c3b9f56 (V51.1) and commits to bucket-2 predictions on that SHA. If the dome author publishes V51.2 with revised parameters before 2026-08-12, our bucket-2 predictions remain correct as a snapshot at the pinned SHA but are not what the dome will publicly claim at evaluation time. The frozen file's anticipated_objections mentions this but doesn't commit to how we will handle a dome-side parameter change between freeze and event.
findings.recommendation: Add a one-line policy under post_event_protocol: 'If the dome publishes V51.x != V51.1 between freeze date 2026-05-02 and event date 2026-08-12, this frozen file's bucket-2 predictions remain registered as the V51.1 commitment. We will note V51.x's revised predictions separately in the post-event evaluation but our trilemma test is against V51.1 at the pinned SHA, not against the dome's most-recent-as-of-evaluation parameters.'

findings.description: registered_predictions_summary table omits B1-G-003 (Sun semi-diameter) and B1-G-004 (Moon semi-diameter). They are inputs, not predictions, so omission is defensible — but for a frozen file, every quoted numeric value should appear in the summary so a future auditor can find them quickly.
findings.recommendation: Either add B1-G-003 and B1-G-004 to the summary as 'inputs' (separate row category), or note in the summary header that semi-diameter values are inputs to B2-G-001/002, not standalone predictions. The cleanup is one line.

blockers_to_ots_anchoring: P2-RP — penumbra radius multiplier inconsistency (visible attack surface, fixable in <10 lines)
P1-NF / P3-OBS-D-02 — undefined noise floor and undefined 'majority of stations' both gate OBS-D-02 evaluability
P1-SIGN — sign convention between B1-M-001 and B2-M-001 ambiguity gates magnetic comparison protocol
P1-OBS-O-03 — non-dome-cited candidate INTERMAGNET stations not pre-listed; OBS-O-03 cannot fire without this list
P3-OBS-D-03 — 'tracks NASA obscuration' not operationalized (Pearson-r threshold or paired test required)
non_blocking_minor_polish: P3-OBS-O-01-VACUOUS (clarify retraction-trigger is symbolic on geometric bucket)
P3-PROJ-CONV (one paragraph on projection convention inside B2-G-004)
cross_pass_synthesis.overall: The frozen file is fundamentally sound. Every numerical claim is sourced; every formula matches the pinned dome SHA verbatim; the symmetric-falsification triad including B3-F-OURS is properly structured. The Pass 1/2/3 findings are real but addressable. NONE of the findings are critical (no typos, no unit errors, no fabricated citations). Six moderate findings are blockers because they are exactly the kind of underspecification a hostile reader will attack post-event; nine minor findings are polish that becomes permanent once OTS-anchored.

holes_found.description: Internal R_p multiplier inconsistency between B2-G-002 (declared 42.4 km), B2-G-004 (uses R_p=42 -> 10.6x, 97.2x), and B2-M-001 (uses R_p=42.36 -> 10.48x, 96.41x).

holes_found.recommendation: Pre-commit to numeric threshold (e.g., +/-2 nT) or procedural definition (2 sigma over 30 quiet days).

holes_found.description: Sign convention between B1-M-001 (positive amplitudes) and B2-M-001 (signed negative) inconsistent.
holes_found.recommendation: Use signed convention uniformly; restate B1-M-001 as [-25, -5] nT.

holes_found.description: OBS-O-03 candidate non-dome-cited stations not pre-listed.
holes_found.recommendation: Pre-list MAB / DOU / WNG / SUA / BEL or similar set.
Replace with Pearson-r threshold or paired sample test at p<0.05.
Drop 'commonly'; commit to hard interval or widen explicitly.

holes_found.description: B2-G-001 derivation '12.00 - 14.32 x 2,534 / 3,199 = 0.658' has rounding mismatch in displayed inputs.
holes_found.recommendation: Add precision to intermediate or round result to 0.66 km / 1.32 km diameter.
Inherit dome +/- error bars or commit to +/-2 nT tied to P1-NF.
Pre-register H-component, baseline-method, peak-window in frozen file.

holes_found.description: OBS-O-01 partly vacuous on geometric bucket; over-aggressive in magnetic overlap zone.
holes_found.recommendation: Clarify symbolic intent on geometric; tighten magnetic to outside-both-ranges.

holes_found.description: Projection convention mentioned only in source_provenance and robustness_check.
holes_found.recommendation: Add one-paragraph projection_convention block under B2-G-004.

holes_found.description: Hartland 88 percent derivation not shown in frozen file.
holes_found.recommendation: Add derivation_notes or archived NASA-calculator URL.

holes_found.description: Dome-version-drift policy not committed for V51.x != V51.1 between freeze and event.
B1-G-003 and B1-G-004 (semi-diameter inputs) omitted from summary table.
holes_found.recommendation: Add as inputs row or annotate header.

stronger_arguments.description: The B3-F-OURS triad (OBS-O-01/02/03) is the strongest part of the frozen file — pre-committing to retraction conditions on our own analysis is rare in the literature and should be highlighted as a structural feature in the post-event publication. The frozen file under-sells this: the rationale section says 'This Mode 5 deliverable is the canary-by-example' but doesn't emphasize the F-OURS arm specifically.
sources: The Mode 5 spec emphasizes symmetric-falsification triad as the structural commitment; the frozen file delivers it but should advertise it.

stronger_arguments.description: The internal_inconsistency_with_dome_PRED_095 note (computed BOU-baseline -10.66 to -12.83 nT vs PRED-095 -17 to -21 nT range) is a sharper falsification handle than the wider B1/B2 tests. Whatever the eclipse produces, the dome's PRED-095 statement and dome's BOU-formula prediction cannot both hold unless observation is in their overlap (which is empty under BOU baseline). The frozen file documents this as a note but doesn't commit it as a B3 discriminating prediction.
sources: 4.2.3 dual-baseline framing (review prose at pinned SHA); dome raw-text 03-predictions.txt PRED-TIER-1 amplitude window; B2-M-001 alternate_baseline_note in frozen file.

stronger_arguments.description: The frozen file is structurally well-designed for OTS anchoring: pinned SHAs in _meta, immutability_notice, post_event_protocol, honesty_clause. The only structural gap is that no in-file commitment exists about WHO will compute the post-event observations and ON WHAT TIMELINE. Decider could publish a separate ots-anchored 'commitment-to-evaluate-by-2026-09-12' file alongside this one — narrows the timeline so we cannot quietly let evaluation slip.
sources: Standard pre-registration practice (e.g., Open Science Framework, AsPredicted) requires both pre-registered protocol AND commitment to evaluate within stated window.

kernel_of_truth.description: Mode 5 as designed is a textbook prospective registration — three-bucket structure, symmetric-falsification triad, repo SHAs pinned, immutability declared. The analyst executed the spec faithfully: every numerical claim sourced, every formula matched verbatim against the dome's pinned formula_runtime.json, every per-station value matched against dome raw-text. The frozen-prediction discipline is the right answer to the dome's wrong-side OTS error (the canary), and the F-OURS arm is rare and admirable. The findings in this review are about polish — not about the underlying methodology.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The OTS-anchoring discipline punishes any flaw permanently. A moderate finding that would be a normal patch in the website prose becomes a permanent attack-surface once anchored. The right answer is to patch all moderate findings BEFORE OTS-anchoring, accept the file might go through 1-2 iterations of analyst+curmudgeon before clean, and treat the polish as part of the freeze process — not as something to defer.

verdict_for_decider.headline: DO NOT OTS-ANCHOR YET. Six moderate findings + nine minor findings. Send back to analyst for one revision pass. Re-route to curmudgeon for confirmation review after revisions.
verdict_for_decider.estimated_revision_effort: moderate (4-8 hours for analyst, ~30 minutes per moderate finding to address with care). Most moderate findings collapse into a single 'pre-register thresholds' section that can be added near post_event_protocol. The R_p multiplier fix is a 5-minute search-and-replace.
verdict_for_decider.after_revisions_re_review: After analyst re-issues a revised EXP-283 to the priority queue, curmudgeon should re-apply Pass 2 (Consistency) — verify the new R_p multipliers are uniform, the noise-floor / sign-convention / candidate-station / quorum / 'tracks' definitions are committed, and that the 'inherit dome SHA freeze' guarantee is unchanged. Pass 1 and Pass 3 do not need to be fully re-run unless the revision adds new claims.

citations_unchecked: NASA SE2026Aug12T path-table 294 km width and 138.2 s duration — relied on 4.2.7 prose; not independently fetched from eclipse.gsfc.nasa.gov in this review run.
NASA Besselian-elements Sun semi-diameter 15'47.0" and Moon semi-diameter 16'16.9" — relied on frozen-file claim and 4.2.7 prose; not independently fetched.
Lunation_no 329 — not independently verified against NASA Saros catalog.

Chapman 1919 / Yamazaki and Maute 2017 / Kim and Chang 2018 amplitude-range claim — relied on B1-M-001 amplitude_caveats; not independently verified against literature.

alternative_view.description: Counter-position: the moderate findings are over-strict. A frozen file is not a paper — it is a prospective registration. Pre-registration practice (e.g., AsPredicted) accepts narrative thresholds ('meaningful signal', 'quiet conditions') because the protocol is what gets evaluated, not a formal definition. By that standard, OBS-D-02's 'per-station noise floor' and OBS-D-03's 'tracks' are normal pre-registration language and don't need numeric pre-commitment.
alternative_view.response: Counter-counter: AsPredicted accepts narrative thresholds because the registered party also publishes a separate analysis plan that operationalizes them. Here the OTS-anchored file IS the analysis plan — there is no separate document. The hostile reader is not a friendly peer-reviewer; it is the dome author who has direct contact with the operator (HNOTE-OPERATOR-AUTHOR-CONTACT-CONFIRMED-001) and will use any narrative wiggle room to dispute post-event evaluation. The OTS-permanence rule means even moderate underspecification gets quoted back at us forever. The conservative move is to pre-commit numerics where possible. The minor findings ARE acceptable as registered narrative; the moderate findings need to be tightened.
